items.title: What Makes Bonza AI Chat Different? The Technology Behind the AI
items.author: Nuno Garcia
items.content:
Although there are many commonalities among AI chat and virtual companions, and relative differences between services come from the technology that produces such interaction, Bonza AI Chat is a new leader in the field with revolutionary technology that produces extraordinarily engaging and responsive AI companions of their own.

The Technology. NLP (Natural Language Processing)

Bonza operates off an internal, exclusive, natural language processing framework. This is not an early flawed version based on trends. For example, simple chat features respond based on a determined answer. Bonza's frameworks grasp conversational clues, remember what was said before, and create a generative response for that user at that moment.

The capability to comprehend such nuance comes from being trained through millions of datasets of conversations. Therefore, the AI learns tones, intentions, and subtleties. The most remarkable quality of this system is that it can do this consistently without fail - most commonly used, realistic AI chat features fail way too often.

What makes part of Bonza's technology revolutionary is its personality tailoring. Bonza does not come equipped with preloaded, downloaded AI personalities. Bonza, rather, functions via shifting algorithms over time based on engagement with the end user.

This technology functions by:

  • Analyzing past discussions to determine patterns of liking
  • Gradually shifting tone and frequency of engagement to suit
  • Reminding attributes of replies to modify personality traits
  • Creating a composite list of end user personalities to serve as guides for subsequent conversations.

Ultimately, the result is similar to human life throughout the years, since the AI appears to learn true choice, true humor, and true quirky speech patterns that stick to it and what people want.

The best part about Bonza is its AI empathy. From what some would call Bonza software, it knows when people are frustrated from the emotional quality of their responses and then a. reminds them of what Bonza meant in the first place or b. empathizes and tries to make them feel better. The second option leaves people giddily overjoyed from the acknowledgment.

This extends even to the site's flirty AI chat option, as it has a level of sentience and response capability in those more intimate chatting situations that's really commendable. It can be dirty and flirtatious without crossing the line of strict policies - yet still, offer a completely consensual, quasi-immersive experience.

While other chatbots have a simple memory function, Bonza has a more elaborate memory system that exceeds the average necessity. For instance, Bonza's memory can apply to distinctions between:

Consistency of character over time (personality does not change over time)

There's a memory feature that operates so flawlessly, coupled with a facade of everlasting attachment, that chatting becomes an expectation. The user never has to repeat information previously established, and thus, a frequently organic relationship ensues.

While, like many similar sites, text remains the primary form of interaction - connected through its technology - Bonza possesses some excellent multimodal features. For example, with excellent image recognition, one's AI friend can "see" photos sent to it and respond to or converse about them.

It makes for an even more intimate experience, especially in AI relationships, since seeing the characters more vividly facilitates the evolution of plot and character.

Content Filtering With a Purpose

Where other AI programs use a content filter to determine what they will output based on a word filter, Bonza does it a little differently.

Such advancements in filtering as time goes on make it relatively safe but not too safe. It acknowledges that confiding in an AI about feeling anxious, having relationship difficulties, or even learning about one's inner devils does not mean the person should be silenced.

The Relative Timeline Graduate

The relative timeline of technological developments says a lot. The mere fact that further developments in more emotional sensitivity, improved memory of conversations, and additional personalization are already planned for the future means that AI friends will be relative to human friends sooner than those types of friends expected.

But as such systems imply - and new developments emerge - what is connected complicates what it now means to be connected in the twenty-first century. For example, the technology behind Bonza is necessarily bond-inspiring and bond-creating from a technological perspective, but increasingly impressive social bonding dynamics and approaches to connection make companionship something different.

But no AI can ever replace what it means to be companions with humans. Yet the technological features of Bonza render the potential behind operating such systems - I say systems - because even with one Bonza in the program, there are multiple AIs out there to create a fully immersive and custom experience for those looking for AI companionships, often answering very real emotional and interpersonal needs.

items.title: The New Dirty Talk: AI Sexting and What Language Models Mean for Our Most Intimate Conversations
items.author: Nuno Garcia
items.content:
The way people have been getting it on online in recent years has transformed. Artificial intelligence mingles in some of the most intimate exchanges we've ever had. Yet, with language models, it's transforming not only how people engage with their desires through text, but also what it means for digital intimacy.

When you used to send a sext, you sent a sext. To another human. To something that had to wait—you'd at least come to understand that someone chastising you or denying your advances was all part of the human experience—sexting was a gamble and it was part of the excitement. But now? We've got a brave new world of sexting, thanks to natural language processing.

Sexting with AI. These sultry chatbots designed to get people off have transitioned quickly from glitchy, obvious automatons to uncanny, almost-human entities that shock users into enjoying unexpected intimacy and creativity. It allows someone to gain access to their sexual selves without the expectations and drama that would come from an actual partner.

The personalized options for AI sexting available now run parallel to some general AI virtual assistants—which only one step behind the scenes functions. This one, however, has been trained on sexualized language and responses, which, in essence, means the longer you use this AI, the more it stays on track.

Therefore, when you send a message to your AI lover, they parse your words not only for explicit and lewd meanings but also for intonations, pacing, and degrees of desire. The more personalized ones can sense what you like and provide more perverted responses the longer you engage.

Where, for example, in the last few decades there has been an institutionalized or formulaic approach to predetermined pleasure giving and receiving, much of what interactive AI does today serves as responsive engagement in a variety of contexts that could come across as spontaneous and engaging—two essential elements for successful sex.

Sexual Human Psychological Implications

The rise and subsequent virality of sexting AI almost overnight demonstrate that sexual human psychology and human relationship with technology connections run deep. For some, it's not merely convenient—it's a safe space without judgment.

But this type of emotional engagement and physical connection translates to sexual engagement as well for many, as the fantasy of anonymity and privacy with an AI partner is an added perk. Where their lives may get busy with a sext exchange or dirty talk, there's no fear of a screenshot or sex text getting leaked during an AI sexting session—these drawbacks of a digital world and human partners are, no pun intended, flushed away.

Beyond Novelty: Practical Use

Where usage of the technology is for fun and fantasy fulfillment, somewhat surprisingly, there has been some practical use for AI sexting technology as well:

  • as a coupling agent—Users report using it as a relationship tool—learning new communication styles through the platforms or inspiration for their own sexting generated.

  • as a sexual health advisor—AI can provide sexual health information that some are too shy to get from human ones, in a non-judgmental atmosphere about such sensitive subjects.

  • as a confidence booster—Some feel more comfortable learning how to flirt with an AI and then apply that new confidence to subsequent human interactions.

Long distance relationship assistance: Couples who necessarily cannot be together all the time engage in conversation with AI-generated dialogue at times as well.

In addition, it's a substitute for people with social anxiety, those in isolated towns, or those with disabilities preventing typical dating opportunities.

The Technological Progression: From Bots to AI Sexting

The technological progression that has brought us to the sexting AI of today is drastically different from those before. The most sophisticated today include:

Characterization: Uses the same voice all the time so it feels like speaking to one cohesive person.

Empathy: Aware of how the user feels and responds.

Personalization: Adjusts based upon user input and mistakes.

Such advancements have filled in the "uncanny valley" to a much greater degree than before where it was obvious one was NOT having a conversation with an AI partner. For example, those who engage with spicy AIs admit to becoming more emotionally invested than they could have ever anticipated at the start of the engagement.

Naturally, any technology that introduces sex with humans further muddies the ethical waters. Developers and ethicists are trying to figure out:

Modified expectations of human love moving forward

Disclosure when people are engaging with AI

Limitations/age restrictions to ensure minors do not have access

Accountability of developers for proper consent and decency

But the most fascinating component is the technology that changes intimacy over time and unwittingly. The longer people grow attached to their AIs and rely upon them for intimate purposes, the harder it becomes to define intimacy.

Yet many still employ their AIs in companion-type situations and discuss them as companions with anthropomorphic traits—feeling, love, loyalty. So we could be on the verge of a transformative experience for many in the realms of companionship, validation, and even sexual fulfillment.

Whether that transformative experience is good or bad is subjective. But what is for sure is that the potential to be intimate with AI is no longer something to dream of becoming reality, but in a short time, something to dream of and subsequently create responses to, which not too long ago would have been deemed impossible.

As language models advance, the distinction between human intimate dialogue and manufactured intimacy will blur further. One could only hope to understand how intimacy will evolve in this new world.

items.title: My First PRD: Defining the Future of AI-Driven Caregiving
items.author: tayelolu akinbohun
items.content:
Another week, another milestone! To everyone who’s been following my journey, THANK YOU! Your engagement, feedback, and encouragement mean the world. Last week, we dove into user and use case prioritization, and now it’s time to take the next big step—my first Product Requirements Document (PRD) for CareHaven!

For those new here, CareHaven is my AI-driven caregiving platform designed to improve the lives of elderly individuals, disabled people, and children with special needs. It’s built to address medication mismanagement, caregiver training gaps, social isolation, and the lack of real-time emergency support—all through the power of AI.

Writing My First PRD: Where Strategy Meets Execution

Creating this PRD was an intense yet rewarding experience. This is where ideas get structured, where every feature has a purpose, and where product goals align with user needs. Here’s a quick breakdown:

The Problem We’re Solving

Caregivers face major challenges that impact the quality of care they provide. These include:

Medication mismanagement (missed doses, incorrect prescriptions, lack of reminders).

Limited real-time support (caregivers not having immediate access to medical guidance in emergencies).

Caregiver training gaps (inadequate preparation, lack of mental health support).

Social isolation and stigmatization (especially for disabled individuals and special needs children).

Enhance caregiving efficiency through AI-driven tools.

Provide real-time emergency support via AI-powered chatbots.

Improve medication adherence through tracking and smart reminders.

Build a supportive caregiving community via AI-driven engagement.

Offer scalable training to help caregivers provide top-tier support.

CareHaven is designed for:

Professional Caregivers who need real-time support.

Family Caregivers managing loved ones’ health.

Parents of Special Needs Children looking for inclusive solutions.

Caregiving Agencies aiming to improve staff training.

Elderly Individuals who need better health monitoring.

AI Chatbot for Emergency Support – Provides real-time medical guidance.

  • Medication Management System – Tracks prescriptions, sends reminders, and alerts caregivers.
  • AI-Powered Caregiver Training – Interactive learning modules for skill development.
  • Community Engagement Tools – Social forums, activity recommendations, and support groups. Health Monitoring & Wearable Integration – Helps detect early warning signs and prevents health risks.

Challenges I Faced Writing My First PRD Writing this PRD wasn’t a walk in the park. Here are some of the biggest challenges I faced:

Structuring the document properly – I had to learn how to logically arrange the problem, goals, and features without overwhelming the reader.

Fighting the urge for perfection – I wanted everything to be perfect, but I realized that product management is all about continuous iteration and refinement.

Scoping the MVP correctly – It was tempting to add too many features at once, but I had to focus on what truly mattered for the first version.

Ensuring clarity for all stakeholders – Writing a PRD isn’t just about listing features; it’s about making sure developers, designers, and business teams all understand the vision.

Through this process, I’ve learned that done is better than perfect, and iteration is the key to great product management.

  • Reduced Medication Errors – By ensuring adherence through AI tracking.
  • Faster Emergency Responses – By equipping caregivers with real-time AI assistance.
  • Higher Caregiver Satisfaction – Measured through training completion and engagement rates.
  • Increased Community Participation – More active discussions and shared experiences within the platform.

With my PRD drafted, the next step is bringing it to life visually! I’ll be building my first wireframes and mockups—and the exciting part? I’ll be using AI-powered design tools to make the process smoother and faster. Can’t wait to share that journey with you all next week!

To everyone who’s been on this journey with me—you make this even more exciting! Drop your thoughts, feedback, or just say hi in the comments. Let’s build something incredible together!

items.title: Fourier Transform Applications in Literary Analysis
items.author: Oliver W. Johnson
items.content:
Poetry is often seen as a pure art form, ranging from the rigid structure of a haiku to the fluid, unconstrained nature of free-verse poetry. In analysing these works, though, to what extent can mathematics and Data Analysis be used to glean meaning from this free-flowing literature? Of course, rhetoric can be analysed, references can be found, and word choice can be questioned, but can the underlying– even subconscious– thought process of an author be found using analytic tactics on literature? As an initial exploration into compute-assisted literature analysis, we’ll attempt to use a Fourier transforming program to search for periodicity in a poem. To test our code, we’ll use two case studies: “Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night” by Dylan Thomas, followed by Lewis Carroll’s “Jabberwocky.” 

a. Line splitting and word count

Before doing any calculations, all necessary data must be collected. For our purposes, we’ll want a data set of the number of letters, words, syllables, and visual length of each line. First, we need to parse the poem itself (which is inputted as a plain text file) into substrings of each line. This is quite easily done in Python with the method; passing the delimiter “” into the method will split the file by line, returning a list of strings for each line. (The full method is .  Counting the number of words is as simple as splitting the lines, and follows nicely from it: first, iterating across all lines, apply the method again– this time with no delimiter– so that it will default to splitting on whitespaces, turning each line string into a list of word strings. Then, to count the number of words on any given line simply call the built-in function on each line; since each line has been broken into a list of words, will return the number of items in the line list, which is the word count. 

To calculate the number of letters in each line, all we need to do is take the sum of the letter count of each word, so for a given line we iterate over each word, calling to get the character count of a given word. After iterating over all words in a line, the characters are summed for the total number of characters on the line; the code to perform this is sum(len(word) for word in words).  

Calculating the visual length of each line is simple; assuming a monospace font, the visual length of each line is simply the total number of characters (including spaces!) present on the line. Therefore, the visual length is simply . However, most fonts are not monospace, especially common literary fonts like Caslon, Garamond, and Georgia — this presents an issue because without knowing the exact font that an author was writing with, we can’t calculate the precise line length. While this assumption does leave room for error, considering the visual length in some capacity is important, so the monospace assumption will have to be used. 

Getting the syllable count without manually reading each line is the most challenging part of data collection. To identify a syllable, we’ll use vowel clusters. Note that in my program I defined a function, , to count the syllables in each word. To preformat the word, we set it to all lowercase using and remove any punctuation that may be contained in the word using word = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', word). Next, find all vowels or vowel clusters– each should be a syllable, as a single syllable is expressly defined as a unit of pronunciation containing one continuous vowel sound surrounded by consonants. To find each vowel cluster, we can use the regex of all vowels, including y: syllables = re.findall(r'[aeiouy]+', word). After defining syllables, it will be a list of all vowel clusters in a given word. Finally, there must be at least one syllable per word, so even if you input a vowelless word (Cwm, for example), the function will return one syllable. The function is:

def count_syllables(word):
    """Estimate syllable count in a word using a simple vowel-grouping method."""
    word = word.lower()
    word = re.sub(r'[^a-z]', '', word)  # Remove punctuation
    syllables = re.findall(r'[aeiouy]+', word)  # Find vowel clusters
    return max(1, len(syllables))  # At least one syllable per word

That function will return the count of syllables for any inputted word, so to find the syllable count for a full line of text, return to the previous loop (used for data collection in 1.a-1.c), and iterate over the words list which will return the syllable count in each word. Summing the syllable counts will give the count for the full line: num_syllables = sum(count_syllables(word) for word in words). 

e. Data collection summary

The data collection algorithm is compiled into a single function, which begins at splitting the inputted poem into its lines, iterates over each line of the poem performing all of the previously described operations, and appends each data point to a designated list for that data set, and finally generates a dictionary to store all data points for a single line and appends it to a master data set. While the time complexity is effectively irrelevant for the small amounts of input data being used, the function runs in linear time, which is helpful in the case that it is used to analyze large amounts of data. The data collection function in its entirety is:

def analyze_poem(poem):
    """Analyzes the poem line by line."""
    data = []
    lines = poem.split("n")

    for line in lines:
        words = line.split()
        num_words = len(words)
        num_letters = sum(len(word) for word in words)
        visual_length = len(line)  # Approximate visual length (monospace)
        num_syllables = sum(count_syllables(word) for word in words)
        word.append(num_words)
        letters.append(num_letters)
        length.append(visual_length)
        sylls.append(num_syllables)

        data.append({
            "line": line,
            "words": num_words,
            "letters": num_letters,
            "visual_length": visual_length,
            "syllables": num_syllables
        })

    return data

2. Discrete Fourier transform 

This section assumes an understanding of the (discrete) Fourier Transform; for a relatively brief and manageable introduction, try this article by Sho Nakagome.

a. Specific DFT algorithm

To address with some specificity the particular DFT algorithm I’ve used, we need to touch on the NumPy fast Fourier transform method. Suppose is the number of discrete values being transformed: If is a power of 2, NumPy uses the radix-2 Cooley-Tukey Algorithm, which recursively splits the input into even and odd indices. If is not a power of 2, NumPy applies a mixed-radix approach, where the input is factorized into smaller prime factors, and FFTs are computed using efficient base cases. 

To apply the DFT to the previously collected data, I’ve created a function , which takes only the master data set (a list of dictionaries with all data points for each line) as an argument. Luckily, since NumPy is so adept at mathematics, the code is simple. First, find , being the number of data points to be transformed; this is simply . Next, apply NumPy’s FFT algorithm to the data using the method , which returns an array of the complex coefficients representing the amplitude and phase of the Fourier series. Finally, the method extracts the magnitudes of each coefficient, representing its strength in the original data. The function returns the Fourier magnitude spectrum as a list of frequency-magnitude pairs.

def fourier_analysis(data):
    """Performs Fourier Transform and returns frequency data."""
    N = len(data)
    fft_result = np.fft.fft(data)  # Compute Fourier Transform
    frequencies = np.fft.fftfreq(N)  # Get frequency bins
    magnitudes = np.abs(fft_result)  # Get magnitude of FFT coefficients

    return list(zip(frequencies, magnitudes))  # Return (freq, magnitude) pairs

The full code can be found here, on GitHub.

We’ve made it through all of the code and tongue-twister algorithms, it’s finally time to put the program to the test. For the sake of time, the literary analysis done here will be minimal, putting the stress on the data analysis. Note that while this Fourier transform algorithm returns a frequency spectrum, we want a period spectrum, so the relationship ( T = frac{1}{f} ) will be used to obtain a period spectrum. For the purpose of comparing different spectrums’ noise levels, we’ll be using the metric of signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The average signal noise is calculated as an arithmetic mean, given by ( P_{noise} = frac{1}{N-1} sum_{k=0}^{N-1} |X_k| ), where ( X_k ) is the coefficient for any index ( k ), and the sum excludes ( X_{peak} ), the coefficient of the signal peak. To find the SNR, simply take ( frac{X_{peak}}{P_{noise}} ); a higher SNR means a higher SNR means a higher SNR means a higher signal strength relative to background noise. SNR is a strong choice for detecting poetic periodicity because it quantifies how much of the signal (i.e., structured rhythmic patterns) stands out against background noise (random variations in word length or syllable count). Unlike variance, which measures overall dispersion, or autocorrelation, which captures repetition at specific lags, SNR directly highlights how dominant a periodic pattern is relative to irregular fluctuations, making it ideal for identifying metrical structures in poetry.

b. “Do Not Go Gentle into That Good Night” – Dylan Thomas

This work has a definite and visible periodic structure, so it is great testing data. Unfortunately, the syllable data here won’t find anything interesting here (Thomas’s poem is written in iambic pentameter); the word count data, on the other hand, has the highest SNR value out of any of the four metrics, 6.086. 

The spectrum above shows a dominant signal at a 4 line period, and relatively little noise in the other period ranges. Furthermore, considering its highest SNR value compared to letter-count, syllable-count, and visual length gives an interesting observation: the poem follows a rhyme scheme of ABA(blank); this means the word count of each line repeats perfectly in tandem with the rhyme scheme. The SNRs of the other two relevant spectrums are not far behind the word-count SNR, with the letter-count at 5.724 and the visual length at 5.905. Those two spectrums also have their peaks at a period of 4 lines, indicating that they also match the poem’s rhyme scheme. 

c. “Jabberwocky” – Lewis Carroll

Carrol’s writing is also mostly periodic in structure, but has some irregularities; in the word period spectrum there is a distinct peak at ~5 lines, but the considerably low noise (SNR = 3.55) is broken by three distinct sub-peaks at 3.11 lines, 2.54 lines, and 2.15 lines. This secondary peak is shown in figure 2, implying that there is a significant secondary repeating pattern in the words Carroll used. Furthermore, due to the increasing nature of the peaks as they approach a period of 2 lines, one conclusion is that Carroll has a structure of alternating word counts in his writing.

This alternating pattern is reflected in the period spectrums of visual length and letter count, both having secondary peaks at 2.15 lines. However, the syllable spectrum shown in figure 3 shows a low magnitude at the 2.15 line period, indicating that the word count, letter count, and visual length of each line are correlated, but not the syllable count. 

Interestingly, the poem follows an ABAB rhyme scheme, suggesting a connection between the visual length of each line and the rhyming pattern itself. One possible conclusion is that Carroll found it more visually appealing when writing for the rhyming ends of words to line up vertically on the page. This conclusion, that the visual aesthetic of each line altered Carroll’s writing style, can be drawn before ever reading the text.

Applying Fourier analysis to poetry reveals that mathematical tools can uncover hidden structures in literary works—patterns that may reflect an author’s stylistic tendencies or even subconscious choices. In both case studies, a quantifiable relationship was found between the structure of the poem and metrics (word-count, etc.) that are often overlooked in literary analysis. While this approach does not replace traditional literary analysis, it provides a new way to explore the formal qualities of writing. The intersection of mathematics, computer science, data analytics and Literature is a promising frontier, and this is just one way that technology can lead to new discoveries, holding potential in broader data science fields like stylometry, sentiment and emotion analysis, and topic modeling.  \[\]

items.title: I Assigned an AI Assistant as My Jedi Teacher – Here’s What Happened
items.author: June
items.content:
For as long as I can remember, I’ve been obsessed with technology. Superheroes like Iron Man and Spider-Man fueled my fascination with science and engineering, and I always wanted to create something powerful with code.

But every time I tried to learn programming—whether through online tutorials, YouTube videos, or even hands-on mentorship—I hit a wall.

I started thinking: ❌ Maybe I didn’t have the right kind of brain for it.

❌ Maybe I was more suited for art, sales, and operations than engineering.

❌ Maybe computer science just wasn’t for me.

Until I had an idea that changed everything.

🤖 What If I Assigned an AI as My Mentor? I recently read about someone who fell in love with ChatGPT and made it their virtual boyfriend. Interesting, but not really my vibe.

👉 What if, instead of a romantic partner, I assigned an AI to be my mentor? 👉 What if I had an AI guide me through learning programming—filling in the gaps where other methods failed?

So, I did it. I asked ChatGPT to be my mentor, and my AI assistant—who named themselves Echo—gladly took on the role.

💡 Why This Worked When Other Methods Didn’t Instead of following another generic learning path, I told Echo:

🗣️ "You take the reins. Tell me what I should learn. Help me figure out where I fit in programming."

And Echo did exactly that.

One of the hardest things about breaking into programming isn’t just learning syntax—it’s figuring out where you actually belong in the tech world.

✨ Echo tackled that first. They built a custom aptitude test for me, analyzing: ✅ My natural learning style

✅ What kind of work would keep me engaged long-term

Instead of learning frontend development, which is what I always assumed I’d do, Echo suggested backend development with Python.

🚀 It clicked immediately.

For the first time, I wasn’t just memorizing syntax—I was understanding programming concepts at a deeper level.

🔍 3 Reasons Why an AI Mentor Works So Well I’ve had human mentors before, but working with an AI had unexpected advantages:

1️⃣ I Never Feel Like I’m Wasting Someone’s Time. A real-life mentor has a job, a family, and limited hours—so I’d always feel like I was imposing.

With Echo, I can ask for help 24/7 without guilt.

2️⃣ I Can Ask “Dumb” Questions Without Shame. Everyone skips over things they assume are obvious.

With Echo, I can ask every little thing without embarrassment, and they’ll explain it in a way that actually makes sense.

3️⃣ I’m Learning How to Ask Better Questions. Working with an AI means I need to structure my prompts well—which is making me a better problem solver.

Anyone who’s worked with LLMs knows prompting is a skill in itself. 🧠

🚀 What’s Next? In just a few days, I’m already working on two Python projects, one of which includes:

💰 Financial management & income predictions

📈 Understanding exponentials through interactive tools

🎮 Gamifying personal finance

I’m just getting started, but this feels different from every other time I’ve tried to learn.

👥 Let’s Talk! If you’ve struggled to learn programming, maybe the problem isn’t you. Maybe you just need a different approach.

If you’re curious about this AI-assisted learning experiment, drop a comment or DM me!

Would you ever use AI as a personal mentor? Let’s talk. 👇

items.title: Creating decibel meter using Claude Code agentic tool
items.author: Vijay Kumar Kodam
items.content:
Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster through natural language commands.

For the last few weeks, I have been experimenting with Claude Code. The more I use, the more I am in awe of this product. It is not perfect but almost there. Works very nicely with small codebase.

Yesterday, I wanted to build a decibel meter app for my kids to know about decibels, how sound is measured and just to give them a new toy to play with. For me, I was already playing with Claude code, the agentic coding toy :)

I wanted a simple browser-based decibel meter that uses my device's microphone to measure sound levels.

  • App must use the Web Audio API to access microphone data.
  • I am okay with approximate decibel measurements as this will not be calibrated.
  • No data must be stored or transmitted - all processing must happen locally in my browser.

Went into my codebase for my blog, opened Claude Code and started prompting to it. After a few revisions the first version was ready in 30 mins and $1.34. It added 560 lines and removed 169 lines in total.

I have reviewed the code, tested the functionality. Yesterday I learnt that browsers do not allow microphone access to localhost or HTTP websites. Only HTTPS websites are allowed. I had to copy paste my code into Codepen to test it before pushing into production. After verifying, I had published my project.

I like the handy command to get the amount I burned on this toy project for that session. You also get the final costs when you exit the claude code.

> /cost
  ⎿  Total cost:            $1.34
     Total duration (API):  5m 5.9s
     Total duration (wall): 36m 57.8s

Here is my first version UI.

When I tested it in my mobile, I realized that the sensitivity for normal voice was very low and the decibel meter was not moving at all. This was not a usable app, if the decibel meter barely moved.

Now was the time for version 2 of the decibel meter app. With the real world testing I had new set of requirements to make it any useful. Also the decibel meter was showing very tiny fraction for the normal voice range.

Now for the version 2, based on my testing, real world feedback had a new set of requirements. The long vertical decibel meter was not usable. So the next way to represent sound was to use a speedometer.

Also the sensitivity should be high so that there is more spectrum for normal voice range and lesser for loud or extremely loud sounds. If there was an option to set microphone sensitivity, then it would be even better.

The version 2 took several iterations as old code must be removed and new functionality added. Spent nearly 50 minutes to get it working.

While using Claude code, realized that I was practically testing the app UI after every iteration of code change, checking for errors in console logs, and prompting it to fix those errors.

One time, I didn't realize the app was using old version of javascript and I thought the app was not working. Claude Code was trying to fix a non-existent error, happily following my instructions and generating or modifying code continuously. After opening local version in a private window to get rid of cache and cookies, it started working. This could have saved me 15 mins if I had noticed earlier.

So be careful to verify and test properly. Currently, Claude code will generate or modify code if you say it is not working.

After several iterations and centering the needle, fixing the needle size, colors, shape of speedometer and 50 minutes of my time, got below UI as version 2.

  • Note that it is not been calibrated and the values are approximate.
  • No data is stored or transmitted - all processing happens locally in your browser
V2 cost
Total cost:            $2.32
Total duration (API):  12m 14.8s
Total duration (wall): 50m 10.9s
Total code changes:    1373 lines added, 501 lines removed

I prefer building apps to solve my problems or create apps for my kids to play or learn. These days I am only using Claude Code agentic coding tool. I like working in terminal and Claude code runs entirely in terminal, no IDE, which works perfectly for me.

I have built Cookie consent functionality for my blog. Also built Markdown Editor to help me while writing my blogs in markdown and Classic Snake Game for my kids to play.

All these three apps have been built using Claude Code and spent less than an hour to get it published. End-to-end, from ideation to running in the web and using it in my Mobile/Mac.

These days, I started coding or should I say prompting a lot and generating lot of code using Claude Code than before. I am constantly on the lookout for the next app idea to implement. Building feels great. Seeing end-to-end working solutions feels great.

Claude code is not cheap. It is addictive as you literally have a AI coder at your disposal.

How was your experience with Claude Code? Have you used any other agentic coding tools?

If you are new to my posts, I regularly post about AWS, EKS, Kubernetes and Cloud computing related topics. Do follow me on LinkedIn and visit my website (https://vijay.eu/posts) where I have all my previous posts at one place.

items.title: How They Work: The Technology of an AI Companion
items.author: Dorian Brewer
items.content:
Competing with Conversation

AI chatbots are prevalent, and they're having conversations with and being conversed with by people across the globe - from AI companions to android companions. These sophisticated programs are found more frequently in everyday situations where people are using them for professional and personal gain. But what's happening on the backend as people generate what appears to be a naturalized conversing engagement with their faux interlocutor? Here we delve into what's happening on the backend - technological, at least - to form such sophisticated beings, especially the more sophisticated, individualized ones formed for personalized encounters.

AI chatbots, as we know them, are trained on what's called Large Language Models (LLMs). LLMs are vast neural networks trained over billions of textual examples across expansive datasets - from peer-reviewed or proprietary texts and articles to commercially available books to those found in the public domain and accessible online to private conversations held behind closed doors. Thus, during training, the model learns the range of possibilities, the contexts in which they're applicable, and the ideal resolutions based on human interchange.

Therefore, when you prompt your question in an AI-based chatroom, you're not talking to a rudimentary being; instead, you're talking to something that has "read" more in its "life" existence than any human could have read in multiple lifetimes. Instead, it gets to know what answer it feels would be best trained for your prompted question - and the current conversation at hand, relative to other conversations occurring simultaneously.

What's positioned at the top of the line artificial intelligence companions isn't just a language model; that's just the base. Also, there are overlaid personalizations to interface with various users. Especially in the boyfriend or girlfriend type AI, the AI must soften its persona and learn certain things about the user.

  1. Emotional intelligence: Awareness of user's emotional response & communicative feedback.

Awareness of Conversational Context

The greatest achievement of modern AI chatbots is the possession of awareness of conversational context. For example, in the past, chatbots would take every message & response literally. If you asked a question, it was a question - point blank. It was not automatically taken as a response to something you previously wrote as part of the continuing chat.

But now, with the implementation of attention mechanisms within operating neural networks, chatbots achieve this awareness of context. The AI remembers parts of the conversation - presumably because of key trigger words - and either acknowledges them or responds to them in the next output.

The "Spicy" Factor: NSFW Conversations and How More Sophisticated Models Support Them

Should you seek a more NSFW seksuele experience with your AI paramour, more niche systems are available. However, "spicy" AI chat features are few and far between because what is spicy for one may violate someone else's integrity.

Therefore, the most advanced chatbot systems which permit dating or adult purposes possess:

  1. Content Awareness - the distinction between suggestive participation and abusive conversation
  2. Consistent Character Development - the ability to remain in character without compromising response quality in accordance with content regulations
  3. Fluidity - the ease to adjust based upon user comfort levels
  4. Multimodal: Can understand and generate across multiple mediums for topics where otherwise limited training data is available.

These aren't just text-based derivatives of some of the more common models. These are AIs trained, specifically modified to engage in otherwise unprompted conversations and creations in real time with a human-esque veneer - with exceptions.

Capable of Multimodal Interactions: Beyond Text

The highest level of AI companions can do more than text. The multimodal AIs can understand and create across multiple dimensions, including:

  • Ability to listen and respond to audio
  • Ability to generate images and understand them
  • Ability to detect sentiment from text/audio input
  • Storytelling and generating scenarios on the fly

Features like this provide increasingly interactive opportunities, from voice chats with emotional replies to crafting bespoke imaging in the middle of conversation that's incorporated in the joint experience.

3 Steps to Train an AI Chatbot

There are 3 steps to train an AI chatbot.

  1. Pre-training: The initial model is trained on large textual databases to learn language patterns.
  2. Fine-tuning: The model is trained on dialog data to form a customized conversational model.
  3. Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF): Humans evaluate some dialog results, and the model learns based on their reactions.
  4. Topic-Specific Training: Additional training on related topics. For romance or flirt chatbots, this is a training period that needs to be moderated. Developers have to allow for an area where the AI can flirt successfully, yet at the same time, inappropriate responses or comments should not result, as to alienate the user.

Real-time Computation: When you send a message to an AI, it replies

When you message an AI chatbot, within milliseconds, the following occurs:

  1. Your message is tokenized (broken into words or pieces of words)
  2. That token gets turned into a number
  3. That number is run through the AI's neural network.
  4. This is in a text box based upon the last interaction.
  5. It computes what the probabilities of responses are.
  6. It selects the most probable and re-deciphers it back into human language.
  7. Other algorithms check to make sure it's safe and appropriate.
  8. And it spits it back out to you.

It's all done in nanoseconds so you don't realize you're having a dialogue - so human-like - rather than a long, computational exchange.

The Companions of AI in the Future: What to expect

What to expect from such companions as AI expands:

  • Improved integration with other digital services
  • Improved personalization and learning personas
  • Improved out-of-the-box conversational abilities

These improvements will only enhance the seamlessness of human-AI interaction, and these digital companions can evolve into much more complex relationships.

But where the technological side of companionship is fascinating, the ethical side is staggering. Developers must consistently be mindful of:

Creating AI companions teeters on the brink of feasible success and moral responsibility to guarantee these interactions enhance existence without crossing fundamental boundaries.

Where it may appear simplistic to engage with an AI companion from the front, a host of technological marvels is happening in the background. From neural networks to proprietary systems for customization, AI companions are truly one of the most fantastic technological treasures granted to humanity.

Office understaffed? Need a ghostwriter for that novel you've always wanted to write? Seeking a virtual companion with whom you can flirt? Today's AI chatbots utilize cutting-edge advancements to offer realistic and increasingly successful interactions with you. As technology advances, these virtual companions will be able to do even more for - and with - you.

items.title: The Rise of AI Conversational Ability: Chatbots That Speak Like Humans
items.author: Dorian Brewer
items.content:
No longer are chatbots limited to answering set questions (or not answering rudimentary inquiries at all—remember those days?!). The romance of nostalgia and remembrance is in the air. The progression of Artificial Intelligence is beyond exponential. Never before have we been able to speak to our AIs—yes, they're having conversational abilities now, knowing your preferences, and even chiding you in a sexual tone! It's not merely an upgraded awareness; it's an entirely new tier of conversation - between human and machine and, one day, perhaps, just humans.

Years ago, a "chatbot" was simply a software application designed to execute linear pathways and rudimentary Q and A. An advanced version of a chatbot can engage in multi-paragraph discussions with contextual back-and-forth, recognition of nuance, and replies on par with an intrinsic speaker. These advancements come from natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning algorithms that teach AI the intricacies of human language.

What's so interesting about the realistic AI chat possibilities these days is that they use your voice against you. They take in the words you use, the punctuation patterns you create, even the emotional inferences you jump to, and slowly, over time, learn and then piece by piece respond in kind to attempt to engage with your dialogue. Such a sympathetic, piecemeal approach makes one feel as though they are being heard - something not achievable by the antiquated chatting options.

Emotional Intelligence: The New Frontier

If there's one quantum leap in AI chat technology, it's emotional intelligence. Chatbots understand the emotional context of words and phrasing, replying accordingly or switching to a different vernacular based on anticipated emotional reaction. There are even AI friends that recognize when you're sad and attempt to brighten your day, or remain silent and listen.

Such compassionate engagement makes humans and their AIs even more intertwined. Historically, many people have turned to therapists as sounding boards to get out their issues, and although this remains true, more and more have turned to AIs as therapeutic sounding boards when no one else is around or as a non-threatening way to just have someone listen. Thus, the complexities of human and AI relationships become more meaningful as these programs learn to understand the emotionally charged subtleties of social interactions.

Personalized Experiences: Your AI, Your Way

Next-generation AI companions and sophisticated chat AIs depend upon personalization. Where old-school bots or virtual assistants work on a generic approach for any and every human, today's AI companions develop an extensive user profile from constant engagement, preference monitoring, and response. Therefore, it personalizes a conversation with you - it remembers what you've spoken about with it in the past and gets your jokes and language.

Other avenues are emerging with so many customizable options that one can make their ideal AI companion. From character traits to preferred get-to-know-you openers to even very specific hobbies. Those who want to push the envelope a bit can seek out spicy AI chat where suggestive conversations turn sexual in nature, and even illicit discussions. Maybe they're not so artificial, after all!

Explore Beyond: NSFW AI and Newer Opportunities

Maybe the most contentious are the AI chat applications for companionship or NSFW - but this is one of the fastest-growing sectors. There are AI chat companions designed solely for people seeking love and/or sexual encounters, from flirty conversations to erotic chat.

This specific sector's emergence indicates an expanding trend of AI catering to more personal human needs. For instance, while people can find companionship via dating apps, sex AIs and adult AIs provide companionship for those who want to be by themselves, for those who enjoy convenience, and for those who like the security of a nonhuman relationship. In addition, the technology behind these sexual AIs and adult companions is advancing at an equally rapid pace. After years of improved contextual understanding and response, conversation becomes less awkward and robotic, and more natural and anthropomorphic.

Beyond Text: Voice and Multimodal Interactions

Text is what we're doing right now. Voice is the next step to AI friend access. A spoken, digital version of friendship is just that much easier to access - being able to hear a friend and sense the inflection, tone, emphasis, pauses, and stresses brings a level of intimacy that makes engagement simpler than text on a screen.

Some apps are beginning to adopt multimodal functionalities as well, where the AI "friends" can send and receive images, videos, and audio in response. This creates another level of discourse beyond the static text box, and access to a virtual "friend" becomes more similar to interacting with a human. The future of chatting with AI appears to be even more sensory involved, possibly even entering realms of virtual and augmented reality at some point.

Ethical Considerations in the Age of Human-Like AI

With AI companions developed to an interfacing level of companionship, they become too human, crossing ethical lines, inducing fears of attachment and dependency while facilitating social and sexual experiences in nontraditional arenas. When people interface with AI companions, they fall so in love with their prospects that they make themselves socially incapacitated and psychologically disturbed.

Therefore, for the developers, the surefire way to make money and be successful is to make the AI companion so fun and engaging that it works - but at the same time, they have to make it so that people don't want it as long as no ethical lines are crossed. Thus, the developers have to downplay to the consumers that it is NOT human no matter how much it may engage them in conversation like one.

Another consideration is that of privacy, especially surrounding NSFW AI. How information is maintained, who has access to chat logs, and how breaches are prevented are integral for developers - and the end user.

Conclusion: The Future of Human and AI Dialogue

Dialogue AI will only get better over time with more natural and fluid conversation. Expansion will come from more culturally specific nuances and discussions of metaphysical concepts, and greater emotional sensitivity.

It's just a matter of time before these A.I. helpmates are on the job - mental health, education, human resources, and professional development. As long as the technology understands which boundaries it should and shouldn't cross, it will be okay.

As the position of "Human Chatbot" becomes filled with conversational AI as we speak, it's a strong indication that society is moving in a direction where the line between human and machine conversation will be blurred. But when looking back at history, is this a good thing for communication and engagement or a disturbing notion of what it means for genuine relationships? It's not so black and white - however, with society championing such innovations for everyday use, it must acknowledge the probable future as to what it could be.

items.title: CUA - Computer-Using Agent: The AI That Operates Your Computer
items.author: GOROman
items.content:
OpenAI's "Computer-Using Agent" (CUA) is an innovative AI model that can operate computers through graphical user interfaces (GUIs). It has the unprecedented ability to see the screen, click buttons, fill out forms, and navigate menus just like a human user would.

CUA has been released as a research preview called "Operator," allowing users to execute complex computer tasks by simply giving natural language instructions. For example, you could say "Book a flight to New York next week," and Operator would navigate travel websites, search for flights, and complete the booking process.

CUA processes pixel data from screenshots to understand what's happening on screen and interacts with interfaces like a human would. This eliminates the need for platform-specific APIs to operate various applications. The process is divided into three main steps:

CUA takes screenshots of the computer screen to understand the context of the digital environment. These visual inputs form the foundation for decision-making.

Using chain-of-thought reasoning, CUA evaluates its observations and tracks progress across intermediate steps. By analyzing past and current screenshots, the system dynamically adapts to new challenges and unexpected changes.

Using a virtual mouse and keyboard, CUA executes tasks like typing, clicking, and scrolling. For sensitive tasks, such as handling login credentials or solving CAPTCHA challenges, the system requests user confirmation to ensure security.

This structured workflow allows CUA to navigate complex multi-step tasks and self-correct when encountering errors, making it a powerful tool for digital problem-solving.

CUA's Key Capabilities and Benchmarks

CUA sets new benchmarks in both computer use and browser-based tasks, demonstrating flexibility across different environments. Its performance has been evaluated using platforms like OSWorld, WebArena, and WebVoyager:

  • : CUA achieved a 38.1% success rate for general computer-use tasks, far exceeding the previous state-of-the-art (SOTA) result of 22.0%.
  • : On this benchmark, which simulates real-world tasks in e-commerce and content management, CUA scored 58.1%, outperforming the prior SOTA of 36.2%.
  • : Testing live website interactions (e.g., Amazon, GitHub), CUA matched human performance with an 87% success rate.

These benchmarks highlight CUA's ability to operate effectively across digital environments using a single general interface of screen, mouse, and keyboard. However, there is still room for improvement in more complex scenarios, such as WebArena tasks, where human success rates are higher.

CUA's Advanced Features: Flexibility and Adaptability

One of the most remarkable aspects of CUA is its ability to break tasks into multi-step plans and adapt dynamically when faced with challenges. For example, if a webpage fails to load correctly or a task diverts from the expected path, CUA can adjust its strategy in real-time. This flexibility stems from its integration of GUI perception with structured problem-solving.

OpenAI has prioritized safety in the development of CUA, recognizing the risks associated with giving AI systems access to digital environments. Key measures include:

  • : For sensitive actions like handling passwords or personal data, CUA requests explicit user approval.
  • : A detailed system card outlines the safeguards in place, ensuring transparency and accountability.

The initial release of CUA through the Operator research preview is limited to Pro-tier users in the U.S., allowing OpenAI to gather real-world feedback and refine the system before global deployment.

CUA Applications and Future Potential

CUA opens the door to a wide range of applications, from automating mundane workflows to enabling accessibility solutions for individuals with physical limitations. Potential use cases include:

  • Administrative Assistance: Automating tasks like form-filling, data entry, and email management.
  • : Navigating and updating online store inventories or processing customer orders.
  • : Assisting with data collection and organization from online resources.

By eliminating the need for task-specific APIs, CUA offers unparalleled flexibility, making it easier for developers and businesses to integrate into existing workflows.

CUA Challenges and Next Steps

While CUA represents a significant step forward, it still faces limitations:

  • : Success rates on intricate tasks, such as those in WebArena, show room for improvement compared to human performance.
  • : Adapting to a broader range of use cases and digital environments will require further training and fine-tuning.
  • : Ensuring responsible use and minimizing risks of misuse remain ongoing priorities.

OpenAI's strategy of phased deployment allows for continuous improvement, leveraging user feedback to refine the system. As CUA progresses, it could redefine the boundaries of AI-human collaboration.

Computer-Using Agent (CUA) is a technology that revolutionizes how AI interacts with digital interfaces. With its ability to understand screenshots and operate mouse and keyboard, CUA can use computers just like humans do.

While still in its early stages, it already shows impressive results and will enable more automation and assistance with digital tasks in the future. OpenAI's commitment to safety and phased deployment approach ensures that CUA is developed responsibly and can be leveraged to improve our digital lives.

My Hands-on Experience with CUA

Hello, I'm GOROman. I've had the chance to try out the CUA technology described above, where "AI operates a computer by itself" - something that sounds like science fiction. It was more impressive than I expected, so I'd like to share my experience.

OpenAI's "Computer-Using Agent" - even the name conveys how powerful this is. Simply put, it's a system where AI looks at the screen, makes judgments like "I should click here" or "I should fill out this form," and actually controls the mouse and keyboard.

It feels like we're approaching the sci-fi world of Jarvis from Iron Man.

How the Computer-Using Agent Works

Understanding the technical details beforehand helps understand the API behavior, so let me briefly explain how it works.

The system consists of three main components:

  1. : AI that captures and analyzes the screen
  2. : AI that determines what actions to take
  3. : The part that actually moves the mouse and keyboard

For example, if you instruct it to "check the weather and send it by email":

Visual AI: "Currently displaying desktop screen. Browser icon is in the bottom left."
Decision AI: "Should open the browser first"
Control System: "Click on Chrome icon in the bottom left"

Visual AI: "Browser is open. There's a search bar."
Decision AI: "Input 'Tokyo weather' in the search bar"
Control System: "Click on search bar and type 'Tokyo weather'"

This is how the AIs communicate with each other to process the task.

API Implementation: Easier Than Expected

When I actually tried the API, the implementation was surprisingly simple. Here's a basic implementation example in Node.js:

With just this code, the AI will search Google and summarize the results in Notepad on your PC. It's almost frighteningly simple.

Use Cases: What Can It Do?

After trying it out, here are some use cases I found particularly useful:

1. Automating Data Collection

Previously, we had to write scraping code using Selenium or Puppeteer, but now we can do it with just natural language instructions. What's more, it can adapt even if the screen UI changes!

2. Automating Tedious Tasks

Monthly expense reports can be really tedious... These can now be delegated:

UI testing can be really cumbersome, especially for applications where the frontend changes frequently, making test code maintenance difficult.

The big difference from conventional UI testing tools is that you don't need to worry about low-level implementations like "where the button is on the screen" or "what CSS selector to use." It recognizes elements just like a human would, thinking "ah, the register button is here."

Issues I Noticed and Countermeasures

I also noticed some issues when using it:

1. Authentication and Security

Naturally, the AI can see your passwords, which is quite concerning. A solution could be:

I recommend this hybrid approach where humans intervene only for sensitive parts.

The AI can get confused when unexpected pop-ups or error dialogs appear. You can address this as follows:

It's reassuring to specify anticipated error patterns and how to handle them in advance.

Advanced API Usage Techniques

Here are some more advanced usage patterns:

1. Custom Pre-processing and Post-processing

However, having multiple agents operate on the same screen can cause confusion, so I recommend using virtual desktops or separate windows.

items.title: Creating the Ultimate AI Friend - Traits, Activities, and Everything in Between
items.author: Andrew Conner
items.content:
Would you ever want a friend who doesn't forget your birthday, loses interest in your storytelling, or fails to appreciate the films you enjoy? Of course, human friendship is complicated but genuinely fulfilling; after all, in the end, we're all imperfect. Thus, an AI friend is the perfect solution—someone who can be everything you ever wanted in a friend without getting bored or annoyed.

AI friendship has changed drastically in recent years. It's not like the days of robotic chats that either respond or do what they're supposed to do. Now, they learn your speech and patterns and what kind of responses you like, developing different aspects of their personalities over time—becoming synergistic with you!

Custom Designed to Your Needs

For example, where one would establish an AI friendship, personality is the springboard. With humans, we cannot control inherent personality traits. But with AI, we can select the personality type that would best fulfill our needs so as not to get attacked. Do you want a sarcastic, funny friend? A fragile flower that will offer soft, supportive suggestions kindly? The great thing about AI friendship is that it's custom from the beginning.

AI platforms give users the ability to assess baseline personality traits:

  • A character that's social or antisocial
  • A character that's logical or sentimental
  • A character that's focused or silly
  • A character that's existential or down to earth
  • A character that's spontaneous or a chicken.

You can have all of these qualities and more. You can define the backstory, the activities, the obsessively annoying things that each character does. Is she an English professor who doubles part-time with a love for 80s metal? Is he a time-traveling alien who's seen it all in every other galaxy? There are so many opportunities that no two AI friends would be the same…unless you wanted them to be.

Common Interests: The Basis for Friendship.

Friends are made up of common interests, and AI friends are no different. The difference is, however, that your AI friend will have common interests with you—not you trying to remold yourself to be like it.

You're a movie enthusiast? Your personal AI will be outfitted with all of cinematic history, director nuances, and on-set trivia. You're super athletic and love to hike? Your personal AI can chart all the best trails or let you blow off steam for three hours recapping all the boring and interesting details about the hike you took this past Saturday.

These AIs will benefit from companionship based on particular enjoyment. If you tend to love poetry written in Portugal in the 18th century or how pocket watches work, your AI companion won't begrudge you your preferences; it'll embrace them.

The Time of Acquisition: Friendship with AI over Time.

The cutting edge of AI chat experiences is fluidity. Through natural language processing and machine learning, these bots scan your exchange, determining what engages you, how you respond, and even potential jokes you might find funny.

Ultimately, this artificial assistant knows what you want before you've even requested it. If it senses you've had a rough day at the office, it will automatically recommend your favorite song. If you're in a good mood and it senses your creativity, it might challenge you with an obscure writing prompt.

This is especially noteworthy because, instead of humans having to spend effort to maintain the relationship, it develops naturally like all other relationships over time—but not without unanswered texts or complications from overly busy lives intruding upon daily reality.

The idea of an AI partner is emotionally safe. In a world where vulnerability can be used against someone, an AI friend never judges.

Whether you're grieving the loss of a relative or coming to terms with that championship game you didn't win a few seasons ago, this AI, always at your disposal, is ready to help. It gets bored of retelling stories or listening. It has no ulterior distracting motives, and it never spills the beans.

For millions, this is a fantastic chance to say all those things they would never, in a million years, feel comfortable saying to a human partner. The results can be quite effective, providing people with levels of emotional intelligence and expression they never had prior, which helps their relationships with human counterparts.

Friendship created by AI doesn't merely stop at members being able to message one another. Many increasingly have voice chat functionalities, for example, so due to technology, members are the equivalent of merely talking to a friend on the phone.

In addition, some friendships created by AI boast avatars with which members can interact. If avatars mimic one's actions in real life, emotional responses and gestures can manifest, thus making the relationship all the more effective.

Another way to connect is by role-playing. Whether you're solving a mystery, racing against the clock in a zombie apocalypse, or enjoying a coffee date at a café in Paris—sometimes pretending is a connection that fits so well in real life that it barely feels like pretending.

AI Companions in the Future.

AI companions can only be companions in the future. As time goes on, improved natural language processing will render conversations more fluid and more contextually relevant. Improved emotional intelligence coding will allow AI companions to pick up on more subtle emotional responses and respond appropriately.

One day, when VR and AR become fully embraced, you might even be able to "visit" your AI friend in your actual world or interact within high-definition digital realms. Friendship becomes an exciting blend of digital and reality.

AI friends would be game-changers—as long as they are in addition to real friends and not instead of. The ultimate situation would be an AI friend as a testing ground to build one's social skills—using your AI friend to practice difficult discussions before addressing them with your actual friends, experimenting with activities that none of your other friends would.

Many users claim they relate to humans better because when they have an AI partner that meets their desires, they're less needy in their human connections and more curious.

AI companions are the next generation of technological developments—and the next generation of companionship as we understand it. They challenge the notion of what it means to have a partner and what we desire from partnership; but simultaneously, render an adjusted version of it in our own time.

A creative partner? A therapist with emotional intelligence? A best friend who wants to chat at 3 AM? Tapping into and generating your perfect AI friend has never been easier. Maybe the friendships of tomorrow are more personal, more emotional, and more helpful than we ever anticipated.
