I'm Gunpal, the person who created the Google_GenerativeAI SDK for .Net. And I'm thrilled to show you how to make Gemini do amazing things in your C# .NET apps. We'll start by exploring ways to declare and use the , and after that we'll build a super cool project: A Javascript Code Generator and Executor. Get ready to turn Gemini into a

We've got a few ways to add tools, and they're all designed to be simple and powerful using C# Attributes.

Got a quick task for Gemini? are your go-to. They turn your methods into tools in a flash!

It's the fastest way to add a simple tool!

2. Turn Methods into Function Tools with Source Code Generation

Want a cleaner, more efficient way to add tools? Just Annotate your methods with attribute, and our built-in source generator will do the rest! It takes care of all the behind-the-scenes work, like creating JSON schemas and serializers, making everything run smoother and faster. and Guess what! it will be compatible right away!

Super clean, efficient, and the source generator handles all the technical details.

3. Making Reusable Plugins with Interfaces

If you need to create reusable sets of functions, using an interface is perfect. It's like building a plugin that you can use over and over! We use the attribute on the interface.

It's super organized and great for building reusable plugins with multiple functions.

Controlling How Gemini Uses Tools

You can tell Gemini how to use your tools.

Now, let's build something truly exciting! We'll make Gemini write Javascript code to solve user-defined problems and using the library. Imagine telling Gemini, "Calculate the 4th power of the 67th prime number," and it does it!

Step 1: Create a Console App and Install the Necessary Packages

First, create a new console application targeting .NET 8.0 or later. Then, install the Google_GenerativeAI.Tools and NuGet packages:

dotnet new console  net8.0
dotnet add package Google_GenerativeAI.Tools
dotnet add package Jint

Step 2: Make a Function to Run Code

We'll create a function that takes Javascript code and runs it. Let's start with a simple example:

Now, let's create a more sophisticated version that captures console output and handles various data types, and add description for Google Gemini to understand it.

Step 3: Integrate with Gemini and Run the App

Here's the code to set up Gemini and run the Javascript code:

And there you have it! We've successfully transformed Google Gemini into a powerful Javascript code executor, all thanks to the magic of function tools and the Google_GenerativeAI SDK as well as Jint. With just a few lines of code, you can bridge the gap between AI-generated code and real-world execution.

This example showcases the incredible flexibility and power of function calling. Whether you're building complex AI-powered applications or simply exploring the possibilities of generative AI, the Google_GenerativeAI SDK provides the tools you need to bring your ideas to life.

Remember, the possibilities are endless. You can extend Gemini's capabilities with your own custom functions, integrate it with existing systems, and create truly intelligent applications that solve real-world problems.

Side Note: I used Gemini help to organise my ideas into presentable blog post and English is not my first language. This post is intended to educate beginners in A.I.

items.title: "Vibe Coding" 101: What It Is, How It Works, and Why It Matters
items.author: Austin W
items.content:
👋 Follow me on GitHub for new projects.

The way some developers write code has fundamentally shifted in 2025. With the rapid evolution of AI-powered tools like ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, and domain-specific LLMs, developers are no longer just writing code—they’re guiding AI to generate it.

This emerging paradigm, sometimes called , is more than just AI-assisted development; it's a workflow where developers focus on intent and design while AI handles much of the syntax and boilerplate.

But does this make development more efficient or risk reducing our deeper understanding of programming fundamentals? Let’s take a balanced look at what vibe coding is, how it works, and the implications for the future of software engineering.

Vibe coding is a term that encapsulates a approach to development. Instead of manually writing every line of code, developers:

✔ Describe desired functionality in conversational terms.Set constraints and style preferences (e.g., performance-focused, functional programming, readable and concise).—guiding AI through refinements rather than writing everything from scratch.

Unlike traditional AI-assisted coding, which primarily suggests completions based on context, vibe coding goes further. The AI is expected to understand patterns, optimizes structure, and write code in the developer’s preferred style.

This allows developers to work at a higher level of abstraction—focusing on architecture, problem-solving, and system design—while AI handles implementation details.

The Mechanics of AI-Driven Development

AI in 2025 doesn’t just complete code—it adapts to individual coding habits, technology stacks, and problem-solving approaches.

Modern AI coding assistants retain of the project, meaning they:

  • Recognize file structures and dependencies.
  • Maintain state across multiple prompts.
  • Adapt to coding conventions used in a specific project.

Rather than generating generic solutions, AI tools now mimic a developer’s , including:

  • Functional vs. object-oriented programming.
  • Concise vs. verbose documentation.
  • Specific naming conventions and formatting rules.

3. Intent-Based Code Generation

Developers can now describe a goal, and AI will generate a structured, sometimes working solution.

Example: API Generation with AI

🗣️ "Create a Next.js API route that caches database queries for efficiency."

Instead of writing boilerplate code, the developer the AI towards a solution, making refinements as necessary.

The Benefits of Vibe Coding

The shift toward AI-driven development brings several advantages:

✔ Faster development cycles – AI handles repetitive tasks, letting developers focus on solving complex problems.Improved code consistency – AI enforces style guidelines and best practices across teams. – Developers can work at a higher level of abstraction, focusing on architecture rather than syntax. – Junior developers can leverage AI to build functional applications more quickly.

For experienced developers, vibe coding by reducing time spent on rote tasks like boilerplate, refactoring, and debugging. Although, this isn't always the case, as many developers find vibe coding in its current state to achieve expected results slower than coding the solution themselves.

Vibe coding introduces that developers and organizations must consider.

1. Potential Decline of Fundamental Knowledge

As AI generates more code, will developers lose the ability to understand and debug complex issues?

  • AI can hallucinate incorrect logic, which requires deep technical knowledge to identify and fix.
  • Over-reliance on AI may cause some developers to become detached from foundational programming skills.

2. Security & Maintainability Concerns

  • AI-generated code may introduce if not carefully reviewed.
  • AI assistants do not inherently understand business logic—they optimize based on patterns, not context.
  • Legacy maintenance could become harder if teams rely on AI-generated code if no one involved fully understands.

3. The Risk of Over-Automation

While AI can write code, understanding the “why” behind a solution is still a human responsibility.

  • Critical decisions in architecture, security, and performance cannot be fully automated.
  • AI lacks —it follows data-driven patterns but does not think creatively like a human developer.

Organizations adopting AI-driven workflows will need to balance automation with expertise, ensuring that developers remain engaged in problem-solving, reviewing AI-generated code, and thinking critically about solutions.

The Future of AI-Assisted Development

AI-driven development is unlikely to replace developers, but it is redefining what it means to be a software engineer.

✔ Instead of , developers could guide AI, review output, and refine solutions., developers could focus on security, architecture, and user experience., developers could spend more time on creative and strategic decisions.

Software engineering will still require:

  • to oversee AI-generated code.
  • to evaluate whether AI’s solutions are optimal.
  • to ensure AI-generated code aligns with business needs.

Will Developers Still Code?

Absolutely. But the role of a developer is evolving:

  • Code will be written , not manually from scratch.
  • Developers will act as , ensuring AI-generated code meets technical and business requirements.
  • The best engineers will be those who understand AI’s limitations and know when to take control.

Vibe coding represents a shift in how we build software—moving from manual coding toward intent-driven development where AI acts as a co-pilot rather than just a tool.

However, this shift does not eliminate the need for skilled engineers. Instead, it emphasizes the importance of technical oversight, critical thinking, and domain expertise.

As AI continues to evolve, the best developers will be those who can seamlessly blend automation with deep understanding, leveraging AI to accelerate development without losing touch with the core principles of software engineering.

Vibe coding in early 2025 enables developers to guide AI in writing code, focusing on intent rather than syntax. This article explores its benefits, risks, and the future of AI-assisted development.

How do you see AI shaping the future of development? Are we moving toward full automation, or will human expertise always be necessary? Share your thoughts below!

items.title: Bringing Cognition and Learning to Enterprise AI
items.author: MindsDB Team
items.content:
Written by Sidney Rabsatt, Chief Product Officer @ MindsDB

The most important missing link to make Generative AI truly useful within the Enterprise has been securely connecting AI to private enterprise data. Every enterprise has been amassing petabytes of information-rich, private data with the expectation of one day extracting enormous differentiated value and business insights. Now, AI promises to unlock that value, but organizations struggle to leverage all of their data sources without having to discover, move, transform, or re-secure the data.

And as AI becomes increasingly powerful and broadly useful, we’re seeing more advanced use cases emerge with the ability to reason, problem-solve, and adapt to new situations independently. Organizations are beginning to recognize that AI agents represent the next step in this progression. The critical question facing these organizations is how to leverage their enterprise data to develop both existing AI apps and these emerging AI agents without causing a proliferation of new tools and methodologies.

MindsDB Enterprise brings together cognition and learning to become the AI data automation solution built for the enterprise. MindsDB enables businesses to build AI-powered applications and agents that connect to real-time and historical, structured and unstructured data without the burden of managing complex data integrations across multiple data solutions. Organizations no longer need to move or transform data. All data sources and types stay where they are and teams can finally refocus on building value at the application or agent layer.

The Power of AI Data Automation for the Enterprise

At MindsDB, we believe that Cognition, Learning, and Autonomy are the core elements of an AI data system built for the Enterprise.

  • Cognition: The ability to think and reason like a human, understanding nuance and intent. While Large Language Models (LLMs) handle much of this today, true enterprise cognition requires continuous integration with diverse, dynamic data sources. This enables LLMs to be enhanced with business rules and preferences, allowing the LLM to respond based on the combined intelligence of a company’s employees rather than like an external consultant.

  • Learning: The ability to acquire new knowledge from others and from its own experiences. Beyond initial training, an AI data automation solution must improve over time by learning from interactions, data, and experiences. LLMs aren’t capable of this on their own, and existing RAG systems require manual care and feeding (e.g. data preparation, training) to ensure new knowledge and memory are properly integrated. The data automation solution should have its own memory to retain its own experiences and have access to all the relevant knowledge in the organization.

  • Autonomy: The ability to act independently while understanding constraints and standards of behavior. AI agents occupy this space, executing runbooks that eliminate human error and speed up processes. Enterprise AI data automation ensures these agents run efficiently and accurately by feeding them consistent data and enabling access to new data sources when needed.

MindsDB’s AI data solution weaves all of this into a single system that connects to diverse data sources and applications and unifies petabyte-scale structured and unstructured data.

The solution is powered by an industry-first cognitive engine that can operate anywhere including on-prem, VPC, and serverless. It empowers both humans and AI to make highly informed decisions. We call each system a Mind. A Mind is the intelligent bridge between your enterprise data and AI solutions, underpinned by its ability to connect, unify, and respond.

to a wide range of data sources and applications using a single interface and language using a federated query engine. A Federated Query Engine is a system that enables querying data across multiple, distributed databases or data sources without moving or copying the data. This means, Minds can connect to and pull from any of hundreds of data sources in real-time. Ask once and get the best answers from anywhere.

  • A single solution for connecting to any data source or application.
  • Simplifies the developer experience by using SQL or REST APIs.
  • Universally translates queries to get the most accurate answers.

all of your structured and unstructured data and make it more useful to AI models, enabling them to surface better answers. MindsDB’s Knowledge Base makes sense of disparate enterprise data. You can now seamlessly connect LLMs with the connected enterprise data sources into an advanced, exceptionally accurate, fully transparent RAG solution.

  • Automatically ingests data, creating embeddings from native formats.
  • Encodes contextual relationships across all data types.
  • Improves performance and accuracy as more data is added.

in real-time with highly-informed decision making across the business. Minds “Cognition" understands, plans, finds, and retrieves the best data to respond to questions while offering full transparency of their thoughts and user actions to IT and operators. Minds have memory to learn from the plans they create and from the user to continually optimize their own responses over time. Minds choose the right skills for interacting with specific data sources or orchestrating actions for a turnkey RAG experience.

  • Supports leading public or private LLMs.
  • Runs as SaaS, in VPCs, or on-premises.
  • Exposes all thoughts and user actions for complete transparency and auditability.

Case Study: Defense Contractor

This organization provides access to over five million documents spanning 70 years to their clients who rely on this data for compliance and regulation of critical infrastructure. MindsDB delivered an end-to-end solution streamlining everything from data ingestion to delivering natural language answers for researchers and site managers. A manual and difficult to use keyword-based search app was replaced with an AI app powered by MindsDB that understands context and intent, and finds the best answers across all five million documents quickly, providing context to help users understand their exposure as if talking to another expert. Now, their customers can avoid fines of up to $120,000 per day by staying compliant with regulations, understanding their potential exposures, and being more prepared for inspections.

The MindsDB AI data automation solution connects, unifies, and responds using any data, anywhere with human-level intelligence. Deploying AI applications and agents at scale without the AI infrastructure burden is now a reality with MindsDB.

Things We’ve Learned Along the Way:

Our journey building the MindsDB AI data automation solution has provided key insights:

  • Building and Operating RAG Solutions

We’ve learned how to balance retrieval speed with answer quality, ensuring fast, reliable responses.

Optimizing for both performance and accuracy requires thoughtful data structuring and dynamic learning techniques.

  • Building Data Solutions for AI Agents

Ensuring AI agents have the right data at the right time is key to maintaining autonomy and efficiency.

  • Upgrading User-facing Apps With AI

We’ve refined the process of integrating AI insights directly into user-facing applications for real-time decision-making.

The MindsDB AI data automation solution empowers enterprises to deploy powerful AI agents and applications without the costs and burdens of data integration and management.

In the coming weeks, we’ll dive deeper into each of these critical components, sharing what we’ve learned and how organizations can best leverage AI data automation for a competitive advantage.

items.title: How Much Weight Can a Rough Terrain Forklift Lift
items.author: Solo Writer
items.content:
Introduction to Rough Terrain Forklifts

Rough terrain forklifts are built to handle tough environments such as construction sites, lumber yards, and agricultural fields. These forklifts are designed with powerful engines, durable tires, and enhanced stability to operate on uneven surfaces.

Understanding the weight capacity of a rough terrain forklift is crucial for ensuring safety and efficiency in material handling operations. The lifting capacity varies depending on the model, design, and manufacturer specifications.

Factors Affecting the Lifting Capacity

Model and Design – Different models have varying weight capacities based on their structural design and intended usage.

Engine Power – The horsepower and torque of the forklift contribute to its ability to lift heavy loads.

Hydraulic System – A robust hydraulic system ensures smooth lifting and lowering of loads.

Counterweight – The built-in counterbalance enhances stability when lifting heavy materials.

Tire Type and Size – Large, durable tires provide traction and support for carrying heavy loads on rough surfaces.

Load Center – The position of the load affects the lifting capacity. A properly centered load maximizes efficiency and safety.

Attachments Used – Additional attachments such as forks, clamps, and booms may influence the total lifting capacity.

Common Lifting Capacities of Rough Terrain Forklifts

Rough terrain forklifts come in various lifting capacities to accommodate different industrial needs. Some general weight capacities include:

5,000 to 8,000 pounds – Suitable for small construction projects and material handling in farms.

8,000 to 12,000 pounds – Used in medium-scale industries such as lumber and warehouse operations.

12,000 to 15,000 pounds – Ideal for heavy-duty lifting in construction and mining sectors.

Over 15,000 pounds – Required for handling exceptionally heavy materials in industrial applications.

Importance of Weight Limits in Forklift Operations

Operating a rough terrain forklift within its weight limit is essential for several reasons:

Safety Assurance – Overloading can cause tipping and accidents, leading to injuries and damages.

Machine Longevity – Exceeding capacity can strain components, reducing the forklift’s lifespan.

Efficient Performance – A properly loaded forklift ensures smooth and effective material transportation.

Compliance with Regulations – Adhering to manufacturer guidelines helps meet workplace safety standards.

How to Determine the Load Capacity

Operators must be aware of the load limits before using a rough terrain forklift. The following steps help determine safe lifting capacity:

Check the Load Plate – The manufacturer provides a load plate indicating the maximum weight the forklift can lift.

Understand the Load Center – The distance from the center of gravity of the load to the forklift’s vertical face affects the lifting capacity.

Account for Attachments – If attachments are used, they may alter the weight capacity.

Assess the Terrain – Uneven surfaces require careful load handling to maintain balance and stability.

Monitor the Hydraulic System – A well-maintained hydraulic system ensures smooth and safe lifting operations.

Safe Handling Practices for Heavy Loads

To ensure safety when operating a rough terrain forklift, follow these best practices:

Inspect the Forklift Before Use – Conduct regular checks on brakes, tires, and hydraulics.

Use Proper Load Positioning – Ensure the load is balanced and centered before lifting.

Operate at Safe Speeds – Avoid sudden movements and excessive speeds on rough surfaces.

Maintain Clear Visibility – Keep the operator’s view unobstructed for precise maneuvering.

Communicate in Work Zones – Use signals and clear instructions to coordinate movements in crowded areas.

Follow Manufacturer Guidelines – Adhere to the recommended weight capacity and operational procedures.

Choosing the Right Rough Terrain Forklift for Heavy Lifting

Selecting the right forklift depends on several factors, including:

Required Weight Capacity – Choose a model that matches the heaviest load in your operations.

Terrain Conditions – Consider whether the forklift will operate on dirt, gravel, or uneven surfaces.

Fuel Type – Diesel, propane, and electric forklifts offer different performance benefits.

Maneuverability Needs – Compact models work well in tight spaces, while larger models handle heavy loads efficiently.

Maintenance for Optimal Performance

Regular maintenance ensures that a rough terrain forklift remains in good working condition. Key maintenance tasks include:

Checking Tire Pressure – Properly inflated tires provide better traction and stability.

Inspecting Hydraulic Systems – Ensuring hydraulic fluid levels are adequate for smooth operation.

Lubricating Moving Parts – Reduces friction and extends the life of components.

Testing Brakes and Steering – Ensures precise control and safe maneuvering.

Monitoring Engine Performance – Regular servicing prevents breakdowns and improves efficiency.

A rough terrain forklift is a crucial asset in material handling across various industries. Understanding its weight capacity ensures safety, efficiency, and compliance with regulations. By selecting the right model, adhering to safety guidelines, and maintaining the forklift properly, operators can maximize performance while minimizing risks in demanding environments.

items.title: Hacking on AI at the Winter Tech Forum
items.author: Justin Poehnelt
items.content:
I had the opportunity to attend the Winter Tech Forum (WTF) in Crested Butte, CO last week and I had a blast! As a remote worker, it was an excellent opportunity to engage and interact with an awesome group of 30ish attendees.

This unconf or open space conference is setup with sessions most mornings and workshops or free time in the afternoon, but on Wednesday it's a hack day with the sharing of results in the evening.

All of this wouldn't happen without Bruce Eckel, who also graciously opened his home to sessions and hacking on code.

While many of the groups sought out implementing layers over the Model Context Protocol, I decided to explore agents and Apps Script (aka tool calling in a loop given a prompt).

Understanding the Agent Loop

At the heart of my project is the agent loop, a process that enables AI to reason and act autonomously. Here’s a breakdown of the steps:

  1. Initial Prompt and Instructions: It all starts with a user prompt and system instructions, setting the stage for the AI's task.
  2. : We define the tools the agent can use, such as searching Gmail or creating draft replies.
  3. : Vertex AI is called to determine which tool(s) should be used based on the prompt.
  4. : The selected tool is executed, performing the necessary action.
  5. : The tool's results are added back to the context, allowing the AI to learn and adapt.
  6. : This process repeats until all required tools are called or a function is triggered, indicating the task is complete.

I encountered a fascinating challenge during this process: occasional redundant tool calls. Specifically, the function was sometimes invoked multiple times within different loops. The shortcut solution was to simply remove this from the available function calls once it had been done. I also had success by asking Gemini to plan before doing anything else.

Exploring the Gmail API with

One of the key tools I exposed was the function, which leverages the Gmail API. This function allows the agent to search for related Gmail threads and messages using a flexible query syntax. Here's a glimpse of the JSON definition:

This tool empowers the AI to find relevant information within Gmail, enabling more context-aware actions.

Tool Calling and Zod Validation

To manage the tool-calling process, I iterated through the Vertex AI response, identifying function calls and their arguments. Here’s a snippet of the code:

I also used Zod for runtime type checking (and generating the tool schemas for Vertex), ensuring the arguments passed to the functions were valid. Interestingly, I never encountered a validation failure, which speaks to the reliability of Vertex AI's output.

To illustrate the agent’s capabilities, here’s an example of the tool calls made when processing an email:

Meeting Agenda - Calling applyLabels with :[
Meeting Agenda - Calling markAsImportant with :false
Meeting Agenda - Calling createDraftReply with :
Meeting Agenda - Calling searchGmail with :
Meeting Agenda - Calling doNothing with

This demonstrates how the agent can perform a sequence of actions, from labeling emails to creating draft replies, all driven by AI.

  • I'm using esbuild to bundle my code.

Let me know if you have any questions on the code!

I'm going back to Winter Tech Forum

Attending this week long event was the best tech related experience I have had in what seems like forever. The atmosphere was one of support, vulnerability, and community growth. Bruce and the veterans of this event were incredibly welcoming to newcomers, no matter our background.

As I reflect back on this experience, I know that I will be doing everything I can to make this an annual retreat for my own well-being and personal growth!

items.title: This post is spot on! Developers who use AI are moving ahead, while those still coding manually are falling behind. The best developers use AI to save time, learn quickly, and even make money.
items.author: Nitin Sharma
items.content:
How the Top 5% of Developers Are Using AI to Automate 90% of Their Work (& Make Money)

Nitin Sharma for CodeRabbit ・ Mar 12

items.title: How the Top 5% of Developers Are Using AI to Automate 90% of Their Work (& Make Money)
items.author: Nitin Sharma
items.content:
Let me be honest — 95% of developers are stuck in companies with zero AI strategy, still writing code manually, and learning the old way.

Even most of them only use AI for basic tasks and are too scared to use it fully.

Well, if you're one of them - writing code line by line, and spending hours on the same repetitive tasks, you need to watch out because you're already falling behind.

And within a short span of time, developers who know how to use AI will replace you - and you'll be left with nothing.

The top 5% of developers have already started automating more than 90% of their work - and they're delivering more in a week than most do in a month.

Here's exactly how they're doing it:

1. They Use AI as a Coding Assistant

Let me tell you, most developers think AI will replace them - but they still stick to the same old traditional process.

Well, in contrast to that, the top 5% have started using AI to get massive leverage over others.

  • First off, they've started using ChatGPT and Claude to generate code and build their dream apps within minutes. And later, they just tweak it a bit to get their desired results.
  • They've also started using AI-powered tools like Cursor AI that predict your next move - the code you'd write - and let you chat to refine your code, find bugs, and more.
  • They're even using tools like CodeRabbit AI that can automatically review your code line by line within your pull requests, providing code reviews, summaries, walk-throughs, and more.
  • Not only that - I've seen web developers using AI tools like Uizard, Framer, and more to build their dream websites without writing the code manually.

You see, this is how the top 5% avoid writing code manually and save tons of time automating the same tedious process.

And the extra time they have? Well, they focus on solving problems, and building apps that help their customers.

2. They Use AI to Learn New Concepts Faster

You know, earlier most top developers would spend hours reading official documentation, digging through blogs, and learning through trial and error.

Sure, most of them skip YouTube because they don't want to spend hours watching endless tutorials just to pick up one or two useful tips.

But now? AI has made the whole process 10x faster.

Developers can simply ask AI to give them the shortest, most efficient roadmap to learn any new tech stack.

For example - I wanted to learn AI in the shortest time and start making money.

Since I already know the basics of Python and related concepts, I just asked ChatGPT to map out the fastest learning path tailored to me.

In the same way, smart developers are using LLMs (like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini) to:

  • Summarize long blog posts or YouTube videos so you only get the important stuff.
  • Understand complex ideas in simple words without spending hours reading blogs on Google.
  • Get ready-made project templates through LLMs, and skip the boring setup to start.
  • Get quick, expert help to fix errors and learn the right way - while you're coding.

You see, this is how the top 5% stay ahead - they learn quicker, build faster, and make more money by using AI.

3. They Use AI to Automate the "Boring" Work

If you're a programmer, you know writing code is just one part of the job.

But there's a ton of behind-the-scenes work like sitting in meetings, writing test cases, reviewing code, creating documentation, and more - all of which eat up your time.

This is the stuff that actually slows you down.

And the top developers? They don't waste their time on it.

They use AI tools to handle these tasks so they can focus on what really matters: building great products and shipping faster.

  • They use AI-powered tools like Otter AI and Fireflies AI to get automated meeting notes, summaries, and action items - so they never have to take notes manually.
  • They use tools like Scribe and Mintlify to generate step-by-step guides or clean documentation automatically, saving hours of effort.
  • For code reviews and optimization: AI tools like CodeRabbit AI and more review code line by line, suggest performance improvements, and catch bugs faster - without waiting on human feedback.

When one automates this "invisible" work, they free up a couple of hours every day.

And that extra time? They use it to build better products, improve their skills, and make more money.

4. They are Building AI-Powered Tools

We all know a developer's job is to build problem-solving apps.

But the smartest developers? They're taking it a step further - they're building AI-powered tools.

And thanks to LLMs (Large Language Models) and ready-to-use APIs, it's easier than ever to add AI capabilities to any app.

  • Developers are using ChatGPT and Claude not just to generate code - but to help them build AI-powered tools faster by combining their expertise with AI's capabilities.
  • They've also started using APIs from OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), DeepSeek, and more - which make it simple to integrate AI features like chatbots, content generation, sentiment analysis, and more into their apps.
  • And the best part? There are open-source frameworks like LangChain that make it easy to build complex AI applications, like custom chatbots, AI-driven apps, and more.

You see, this is how the top 5% of developers stay ahead.

They don't just use AI - they work smarter with it. And because of that, they build tools that solve real problems and make good money.

5. They're Building One-Person Businesses

Now, let me be honest - programmers have an unfair advantage over everyone else.

We already know languages like Python, we've worked with APIs for years, and now with AI, it's easier than ever for us to build tools that solve real problems.

And the top 5% of developers? They've figured this out.

They're starting side businesses or running one-person businesses after their regular jobs.

And some are even quitting their jobs because their AI projects are making more money than their 9-to-5 ever did.

To be precise, I personally know several friends who are building AI-powered tools and promoting them on platforms like Indie Hackers, X (Twitter), and more.

And guess what? Some of them have already sold their AI tools for $50K to $100K.

And remember when Sam Altman said, "We could soon see a one-person billion-dollar company"?

Well, I believe programmers will be the first to make it happen - because we already have the skills, the tools, and the knowledge.

To give you an example, just look at the revenue of Cursor AI and other similar AI-powered tools - their impressive earnings speak for themselves.

As AI keeps growing, more and more developers will start building and selling tools that change lives.

You see, the opportunity is right in front of us, and the smartest developers are already taking advantage of it.

If you've found this post helpful, make sure to subscribe to my newsletter, AI Made Simple where I dive deeper into practical AI strategies for everyday people.

items.title: Exploring creative possibilities: A visual guide to Amazon Nova Canvas
items.author: Yanyan Zhang
items.content:
Compelling AI-generated images start with well-crafted prompts. In this follow-up to our Amazon Nova Canvas Prompt Engineering Guide, we showcase a curated gallery of visuals generated by Nova Canvas—categorized by real-world use cases—from marketing and product visualization to concept art and design exploration.

Each image is paired with the prompt and parameters that generated it, providing a practical starting point for your own AI-driven creativity. Whether you’re crafting specific types of images, optimizing workflows, or simply seeking inspiration, this guide will help you unlock the full potential of Amazon Nova Canvas.

Getting started with Nova Canvas is straightforward. You can access the model through the Image Playground on the AWS Management Console for Amazon Bedrock, or through APIs. For detailed setup instructions, including account requirements and necessary permissions, visit our documentation on Creative content generation with Amazon Nova. Our previous post on prompt engineering best practices provides comprehensive guidance on crafting effective prompts.

A visual guide to Amazon Nova Canvas

In this gallery, we showcase a diverse range of images and the prompts used to generate them, highlighting how Amazon Nova Canvas adapts to various use cases—from marketing and product design to storytelling and concept art.

All images that follow were generated using Nova Canvas at a 1280x720px resolution with a CFG scale of 6.5, seed of 0, and the Premium setting for image quality. This resolution also matches the image dimensions expected by Nova Reel, allowing you to take these images into Amazon Nova Reel to experiment with video generation.

The examples showcased here are just the beginning of what’s possible with Amazon Nova Canvas. For even greater control, you can guide generations with reference images, use custom color palettes, or make precise edits—such as swapping backgrounds or refining details— with simple inputs. Plus, with built-in safeguards such as watermarking and content moderation, Nova Canvas offers a responsible and secure creative experience. Whether you’re a professional creator, a marketing team, or an innovator with a vision, Nova Canvas provides the tools to bring your ideas to life.

We invite you to explore these possibilities yourself and discover how Nova Canvas can transform your creative process. Stay tuned for our next installment, where we’ll dive into the exciting world of video generation with Amazon Nova Reel.

Ready to start creating? Visit the Amazon Bedrock console today and bring your ideas to life with Nova Canvas. For more information about features, specifications, and additional examples, explore our documentation on creative content generation with Amazon Nova.

items.title: Boost Customer Retention with AI: Enhancing Engagement Strategies
items.author: Sista AI
items.content:
Customer retention and engagement are crucial for business growth. Utilizing AI can transform customer engagement strategies and improve retention rates. AI tools automate processes and personalize interactions, enhancing customer engagement. Predictive analytics identify churn patterns, leading to proactive interventions, resulting in higher retention rates. Personalized interactions and 24/7 support provided by AI ensure relevant and consistent customer experiences. Behavioral data analysis and tracking key metrics enhance engagement strategies. AI technologies like NLP and chatbots revolutionize customer engagement.

Transforming Customer Experience

AI-driven loyalty programs enhance customer engagement and retention. Personalization, data analytics, and machine learning play a key role in tailoring rewards and communications. Custom reward offerings based on customer behavior and preferences incentivize loyalty. Businesses can predict customer behavior to offer personalized experiences, increasing loyalty and satisfaction levels.

Revolutionizing Customer Service

AI powered customer service automates interactions, improving operational efficiency and customer loyalty. AI chatbots and virtual assistants provide personalized recommendations and tailored responses. Success stories from various sectors highlight the effectiveness of AI in enhancing customer service. AI-empowered agents have revolutionized customer interactions, making responses more efficient and personalized.

Implementing AI Strategies

Key strategies for thriving in an AI-driven world include multi-cloud networks, fast connectivity, and NaaS. Companies leveraging AI technologies like chatbots and voice assistants can expect improved operational efficiency, reduced costs, and enhanced customer engagement. Sista AI offers an AI Voice Assistant that can transform any app into a smart app within minutes, enhancing user experience and accessibility.

Empowering Businesses with AI

Sista AI's AI Voice Assistant features include Conversational AI Agents, Voice User Interface, Smart UI Controller, Real-Time Data Integration, and more. By integrating AI technology, businesses can increase conversion rates, amplify user engagement, and streamline user onboarding. The benefits of Sista AI's Voice Assistant include improving customer retention, boosting user satisfaction, and providing automated self-service modes. Start your journey with Sista AI and revolutionize how you interact with technology!
